headline: Honda Recalls 12,600 Off-Road Motorcycles Over Handlebar Clamp Safety Risk

image.caption: Recalled Honda Off-Road Motorcycle CRF250R

description: American Honda recalls 2025-2026 CRF250R and CRF450R off-road motorcycles due to handlebar clamp defect posing crash hazard risk.
keywords: Model Year 2025-2026 CRF250R/RX/RWE and CRF450R/RX/RWE Off-Road Motorcycles
The handlebar's clamp can unexpectedly cause the handlebar to rotate, posing a risk of serious injury or death due to a crash hazard.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: American Honda is recalling about 12,600 model year 2025-2026 CRF250R/RX/RWE and CRF450R/RX/RWE off-road motorcycles because a defective handlebar clamp can cause the handlebar to rotate unexpectedly, increasing the risk of a crash. Consumers should stop using these motorcycles immediately and contact an authorized Honda Powersports dealer for a free inspection and repair or replacement of the clamp.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
This recall involves model year 2025-2026 Honda off-road motorcycles with model numbers CRF250R, CRF250RX, CRF250RWE, CRF450R, CRF450RX, and CRF450RWE. These motorcycles feature a red chassis and have the white Honda logo printed on both sides of the rear. The model number is displayed in white letters on the front of the bike. The vehicle identification number (VIN) is located on the left side of the steering head.

About 12,600 units were sold nationwide through authorized Honda Powersports dealers from June 2024 through March 2026, priced between $8,900 and $13,200. Additionally, about six units were sold in Canada. The motorcycles were imported by American Honda Motor Co. Inc., based in Torrance, California, and manufactured in Japan.

The recall was announced on April 30, 2026, after the firm received one report of a crash related to the handlebar clamp defect. No injuries have been reported to date.

products.NumberOfUnits: About 12,600 (In addition, about six were sold in Canada)

hazards: The handlebar's clamp can unexpectedly cause the handlebar to rotate, posing a risk of serious injury or death due to a crash hazard.
remedies: Consumers should stop using the recalled off-road motorcycles immediately and contact an authorized Honda Powersports dealer to schedule an appointment for a free inspection and if necessary, a replacement of the handlebar's clamp at the dealer's location.
injuries: The firm has received one report of a crash. No injuries have been reported.
retailers: Authorized Honda Powersports dealers nationwide from June 2024 through March 2026 for between $8,900 and $13,200.
importers: American Honda Motor Co. Inc, of Torrance, California

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
To determine if your motorcycle is part of this recall, check the following:

  • Model Number: Located on the front of the bike in white letters. The affected models are CRF250R, CRF250RX, CRF250RWE, CRF450R, CRF450RX, and CRF450RWE from model years 2025 and 2026.
  • Appearance: The motorcycles have a red chassis and a white Honda logo printed on both sides of the rear.
  • VIN Location: The vehicle identification number (VIN) is on the left side of the steering head. You can use the VIN to verify if your motorcycle is included in the recall by visiting Honda’s recall website.

content.subtitle: What Consumers Should Do
content.text: If you own one of the recalled motorcycles, stop using it immediately to avoid the risk of a crash. Contact an authorized Honda Powersports dealer to schedule a free inspection. If the handlebar clamp is found to be defective, the dealer will repair or replace the clamp at no cost to you. For more information or to check your VIN, visit Honda’s official recall page or call American Honda Motor toll-free at 866-784-1870, Monday through Friday from 8:30 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. PT.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: The handlebar clamp on these recalled motorcycles can unexpectedly allow the handlebar to rotate while riding. This sudden rotation can cause the rider to lose control, increasing the risk of a crash that could result in serious injury or death. The defect affects the stability and handling of the motorcycle, making it unsafe to operate until repaired.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: This recall affects owners of 2025-2026 Honda CRF250R, CRF250RX, CRF250RWE, CRF450R, CRF450RX, and CRF450RWE off-road motorcycles. These motorcycles were sold exclusively through authorized Honda Powersports dealers nationwide and a small number in Canada. If you own one of these models, your motorcycle may be included in this recall.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: American Honda Motor toll-free at 866-784-1870 from 8:30 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. PT Monday through Friday, or online at https://powersports.honda.com and click on "Recalls" at the top of the page for more information or at https://powersports.honda.com/recalls.

faq.question: How can I check if my Honda off-road motorcycle is included in this recall?
faq.answer: You can check your motorcycle’s VIN, located on the left side of the steering head, against the recall list on Honda’s official recall website or contact an authorized Honda Powersports dealer for assistance.

faq.question: What is the safety hazard with the recalled motorcycles?
faq.answer: The handlebar clamp can unexpectedly allow the handlebar to rotate while riding, which may cause the rider to lose control and crash, posing a risk of serious injury or death.

faq.question: Will Honda repair the recalled motorcycles for free?
faq.answer: Yes, authorized Honda Powersports dealers will inspect and repair or replace the defective handlebar clamp free of charge.

headline: Build-A-Bear Recalls Heartwarming Hugs Bears Over Zipper Choking Risk

image.caption: Recalled Heartwarming Hugs Bear

description: Build-A-Bear Workshop recalls Heartwarming Hugs Bears due to detachable zipper slider posing choking hazard to children. About 36,000 units affected.
keywords: The zipper slider on the side pouch can detach, posing a risk of serious injury or death from choking hazard to children.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: Build-A-Bear Workshop is recalling about 36,000 Heartwarming Hugs weighted plush bears because the zipper slider on the side pouch can detach, posing a choking hazard to children. Consumers should stop using the bear immediately and return it to a Build-A-Bear store or contact the company for a refund or replacement.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
This recall involves the Build-A-Bear Workshop Heartwarming Hugs weighted plush bear, model number 034464. The bear features a side pouch containing a heart-shaped bag filled with 2.5 pounds of ceramic beads that can be heated or cooled for comfort. The pouch is secured with a zipper located on the side of the bear.

About 36,000 units were sold in the United States and an additional 520 in Canada from January 2026 through March 2026 at Build-A-Bear Workshop stores and online at www.buildabear.com for approximately $48. The product is intended for children aged 3 years and older and includes a cautionary statement advising adult supervision due to the heated/cooled element.

There has been one report from the United Kingdom of the zipper slider detaching during use, but no injuries or incidents have been reported in the United States.

products.Name: Build-A-Bear Workshop Heartwarming Hugs Bears
products.NumberOfUnits: About 36,000 (In addition 520 were sold in Canada)

hazards: The zipper slider on the side pouch can detach, posing a risk of serious injury or death from choking hazard to children.
remedies: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled Heartwarming Hugs Bear and return it to a local Build-A-Bear Workshop store to receive a refund in the form of original payment or a gift card for the original purchase price redeemable toward any available Make-Your-Own furry friend and any other available merchandise. If a consumer is unable to return their Heartwarming Hugs Bear to a local store, they can visit www.buildabear.com/recalls for more information or call Build-A-Bear to receive a free return shipping label to return the recalled bear and to receive a refund check in the form of original payment or in the form of a digital gift card to use on the firm's website.
injuries: There has been one consumer report in the United Kingdom of the zipper slider detaching while in use, and no reports in the United States. No injuries have been reported.
retailers: Build-A-Bear Workshop stores and online at www.buildabear.com from January 2026 through March 2026 for about $48.
importers: Build-A-Bear Workshop, Inc., of St Louis, Missouri

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text: Consumers should look for the Heartwarming Hugs weighted plush bear with model number 034464. The model number is located on the label sewn into the back side of one of the bear's legs. The bear has a side pouch secured with a zipper containing a heart-shaped bag filled with ceramic beads. The pouch zipper slider is the part that can detach and cause the hazard.

content.subtitle: What Consumers Should Do
content.text:
Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled Heartwarming Hugs Bear. They can return the bear to any local Build-A-Bear Workshop store to receive a refund in the form of the original payment or a gift card redeemable toward any Make-Your-Own furry friend or other merchandise.

If returning the bear to a store is not possible, consumers can visit www.buildabear.com/recalls or call Build-A-Bear at 844-541-0144 to request a free return shipping label. After returning the bear by mail, consumers will receive a refund check or a digital gift card for the original purchase price.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: The hazard involves the zipper slider on the side pouch of the Heartwarming Hugs Bear. This zipper slider can detach, creating small parts that pose a choking risk to young children. If a child puts the detached zipper slider in their mouth, it could lead to serious injury or death from choking. The risk is particularly concerning for children under three years old, although the product is labeled for ages three and up.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: This recall affects consumers who purchased the Heartwarming Hugs weighted plush bear, model number 034464, from Build-A-Bear Workshop stores or online between January and March 2026. The product is intended for children aged 3 years and older but poses a choking hazard if the zipper slider detaches.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Build-A-Bear at 844-541-0144 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT Monday through Saturday and 7 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. CT on Sunday or by email at ProductHotline@buildabear.com, or online at https://www.buildabear.com/recalls or https://www.buildabear.com and click on "Recall" at the bottom of the page for more information.

faq.question: What specific part of the Heartwarming Hugs Bear is hazardous?
faq.answer: The zipper slider on the side pouch of the Heartwarming Hugs Bear can detach, creating a choking hazard for children.

faq.question: How can I identify if my bear is part of the recall?
faq.answer: Check the label sewn into the back side of one bear leg for model number 034464. The bear has a side pouch with a zipper containing a heart-shaped bag filled with ceramic beads.

faq.question: What should I do if I have a recalled Heartwarming Hugs Bear?
faq.answer: Stop using the bear immediately and return it to a Build-A-Bear Workshop store for a refund or contact Build-A-Bear to arrange a mail return and refund.

headline: Recall Alert: Stephan Baby Boo Bunnie and Friends Toys Pose Serious Injury Risks

image.caption: Recalled Stephan Baby Boo Bunnie and Friends Children's Toys

description: CBC Group recalls Stephan Baby Boo Bunnie and Friends children's toys due to choking and laceration hazards from breakable plastic cubes. About 227,500 units affected.
keywords: Stephan Baby Boo Bunnie and Friends Children's Toys
The toys' plastic cube can break into small parts or sharp edges, posing a risk of serious injury from choking and laceration hazards.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: The Stephan Baby Boo Bunnie and Friends children's toys include a removable plastic cube that can break into small, sharp pieces, posing choking and laceration risks. Consumers should stop using the cube immediately and contact Stephan Baby for a refund or store credit.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
This recall involves all Stephan Baby-branded Boo Bunnie and Friends children's toys. These toys are small plush animals featuring a removable, water-filled plastic cube designed to be chilled for comfort. The toys measure approximately 3.5 to 3.7 inches long, 2.5 to 3 inches tall, and 2.3 to 2.5 inches wide. They were sold in various colors and animal shapes.

The recalled toys were sold nationwide at independent stores and online through retailers including Stephanbaby.com, Amazon.com, Walmart.com, and Target.com from August 2017 through January 2026, with prices ranging from $2 to $9. About 227,500 units were sold in the U.S., with an additional 1,400 sold in Canada.

The hazard arises from the toys' plastic cube, which can crack or break into small parts or sharp edges, creating choking and laceration dangers. The U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) has received 20 reports of the cube breaking, including one incident where a child swallowed sharp pieces.

Consumers with proof of purchase will receive a full refund. Consumers without proof of purchase will receive a $10 refund in the form of a check or a $20 stephanbaby.com store credit. Consumers will be asked to write in permanent marker "Recalled" on the indented area of the cube and upload a photo of the toy and the marked cube to https://recall.stephanbaby.com/ to receive a pre-paid shipping return label for the cube.

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
To identify the recalled toys, look for the following details:

  • The product is a small plush animal measuring about 3.5 to 3.7 inches long, 2.5 to 3 inches tall, and 2.3 to 2.5 inches wide.
  • The toy includes a removable, water-filled plastic cube designed to be chilled.
  • "Stephan BABY" is printed on a sewn-in label located along the side seam on the right underside of the plush toy.
  • The same "Stephan BABY" text appears on the front and underside of the toy's box packaging.

Refer to the product packaging and sewn-in label to confirm if your toy is part of this recall.

content.subtitle: What Consumers Should Do
content.text:
If you have a recalled Stephan Baby Boo Bunnie and Friends toy, immediately stop using the plastic cube and keep it away from children. Consumers should contact Stephan Baby to receive a refund or store credit.

Consumers with proof of purchase will receive a full refund. Those without proof of purchase can receive a $10 refund by check or a $20 store credit for stephanbaby.com.

Consumers will be asked to write "Recalled" in permanent marker on the indented area of the cube and upload a photo of the toy and marked cube at https://recall.stephanbaby.com/ to receive a prepaid shipping label to return the cube.

Contact CBC Group toll-free at 877-725-5591 from 7 a.m. to 3 p.m. PT or email boobunnie@stephanbaby.com for assistance.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: The removable plastic cube inside the Stephan Baby Boo Bunnie and Friends toys can break into small, sharp fragments. These fragments pose a serious risk because young children might choke on the small parts or suffer cuts from the sharp edges. The risk is especially concerning for children who tend to put toys or parts of toys in their mouths.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: This recall affects consumers who have purchased Stephan Baby Boo Bunnie and Friends children's toys. These toys were sold nationwide and online from 2017 to early 2026. Families with young children who own these plush toys with the removable plastic cube should carefully check their products.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: CBC Group, toll-free at 877-725-5591 from 7 a.m. to 3 p.m. PT, email at boobunnie@stephanbaby.com or online at https://recall.stephanbaby.com/ or https://stephanbaby.com/ and click on "Safety Recalls" at the bottom of the page for more information.

faq.question: What specific hazard does the recalled Stephan Baby toy pose?
faq.answer: The removable plastic cube inside the toy can break into small, sharp pieces, posing choking and laceration hazards to children.

faq.question: How can I tell if my Stephan Baby Boo Bunnie toy is part of the recall?
faq.answer: Check for the "Stephan BABY" label sewn along the side seam on the right underside of the plush and on the front and underside of the toy’s packaging. The toy is a small plush animal with a removable, water-filled plastic cube.

faq.question: What should I do if I have a recalled toy but no proof of purchase?
faq.answer: You can still receive a $10 refund by check or a $20 store credit for stephanbaby.com by contacting Stephan Baby and following the return instructions.

headline: Recall of Travel Size Afrin® Original Nasal Spray 6 mL Bottles for Non-Child-Resistant Packaging

image.caption: Recalled Afrin® 6mL Size Original Nasal Spray Bottles (Front of Bottle)

description: Bayer recalls about 786,100 travel size Afrin® Original Nasal Spray 6 mL bottles due to risk of child poisoning from non-child-resistant packaging.
keywords: Travel Size Afrin® Original Nasal Spray 6 mL Bottles

The 6 mL nasal sprays contain an imidazoline, which must be in child-resistant packaging or meet the labeling requirements for non-complying packaging, as required by the Poison Prevention Packaging Act. The 6 mL nasal spray's packaging is not child-resistant nor bears the required labeling statement, posing a risk of serious injury or illness from poisoning, if the contents are swallowed by young children.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: Bayer is recalling approximately 786,100 travel size Afrin® Original Nasal Spray 6 mL bottles because their packaging is not child-resistant as required, posing a poisoning risk to young children if swallowed.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
This recall involves Travel Size Afrin® Original Nasal Spray 6 mL bottles with specific lot numbers: 230361, 240822, 241198, 250066, 250152, 250646, and 250831. These bottles have "Afrin® Original Nasal Spray" and "1/5 FL OZ (6 mL)" printed on the front label. No other sizes of Afrin nasal spray are included in this recall.

About 786,100 units were sold from September 2024 through April 2026 at convenience stores and travel hubs such as airports, priced between $7 and $9. The product was manufactured by Bayer HealthCare LLC in Whippany, New Jersey, with manufacturing origins in Germany.

No injuries have been reported related to this recall.

products.Name: Travel Size Afrin® Original Nasal Spray 6 mL Bottles

hazards: The 6 mL nasal sprays contain an imidazoline, which must be in child-resistant packaging or meet the labeling requirements for non-complying packaging, as required by the Poison Prevention Packaging Act. The 6 mL nasal spray's packaging is not child-resistant nor bears the required labeling statement, posing a risk of serious injury or illness from poisoning, if the contents are swallowed by young children.
remedies: Consumers should immediately secure the recalled bottles out of sight and reach of children and go to the webform posted on www.livewell.bayer.com/afrin-original-spray-recall to begin a request for a refund. Consumers will be asked to take and submit a photo of the product before disposing of it in order to receive a refund.
retailers: Retailers such as convenience stores and travel hubs including airports from September 2024 to April 2026 for about $7 to $9.
manufacturers: Bayer HealthCare LLC, of Whippany, New Jersey

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
To identify if you have a recalled product, check the front label of the nasal spray bottle for the following details:

  • Brand: Afrin® Original Nasal Spray
  • Size: 6 mL (also labeled as 1/5 FL OZ)
  • Lot number printed as a six-digit code on the bottle, matching one of these: 230361, 240822, 241198, 250066, 250152, 250646, or 250831
  • Expiration date printed in the format "YYYYMMM" following the lot number

Only these travel size 6 mL bottles with the above lot numbers are included in the recall. Other Afrin sizes or products are not affected.

content.subtitle: What Consumers Should Do
content.text:
If you have a recalled Afrin® Original Nasal Spray 6 mL bottle, immediately secure it out of reach and sight of children. Then, visit the refund webform at www.livewell.bayer.com/afrin-original-spray-recall to request a refund. You will be asked to submit a photo of the product as part of the refund process. After submitting your request, you should dispose of the product safely.

For assistance, you can contact Bayer toll free at 800-317-2165, Monday through Friday, 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: The recalled 6 mL Afrin nasal sprays contain an active ingredient called imidazoline. By law, products containing this chemical must be packaged in child-resistant containers or carry specific labeling to prevent accidental poisoning. These recalled bottles do not have child-resistant packaging nor the required warning labels. This creates a risk that young children could swallow the contents, leading to serious injury or illness from poisoning.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: This recall affects consumers who have purchased the travel size 6 mL Afrin® Original Nasal Spray bottles with the specified lot numbers. These products were sold primarily at convenience stores and travel locations such as airports between September 2024 and April 2026. If you have this product in your home, especially if children are present, you should take action.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Bayer toll free at 800 317-2165, Monday through Friday 8 a.m. through 8 p.m. ET, or online through a recall refund webform available at www.livewell.bayer.com/afrin-original-spray-recall. The form can also be accessed by clicking the "Safety Recall Info" button located at the top right of the landing page www.afrin.com, which will take the consumer to the recall information page.

faq.answer: The recalled 6 mL Afrin nasal spray bottles lack child-resistant packaging and required labeling, which increases the risk of poisoning if young children swallow the contents.

faq.question: Which lot numbers of Afrin nasal spray are included in the recall?
faq.answer: The recall includes bottles with lot numbers 230361, 240822, 241198, 250066, 250152, 250646, and 250831.

faq.question: Where were the recalled Afrin nasal sprays sold?
faq.answer: They were sold at convenience stores and travel hubs such as airports from September 2024 through April 2026.

headline: Qumeney Bicycle Light Sets Recalled Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Button Cell Battery Ingestion

image.caption: Recalled Qumeney Bicycle Light Sets – 24-Pack

description: Qumeney Bicycle Light Sets sold on Amazon are recalled due to accessible button cell batteries posing ingestion hazards to children. Refund available.
keywords: The recalled bicycle lights violate the mandatory standard for consumer products with button cell and coin batteries because they contain button cell batteries that can be accessed easily by children, posing an ingestion hazard. Additionally, the bicycle lights do not have the warnings required under Reese's Law. When button cell or coin batteries are swallowed, the ingested batteries can cause serious injuries, internal chemical burns, and death.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: The Qumeney Bicycle Light Sets, sold on Amazon by Smfanlus, are recalled because their button cell batteries can be easily accessed by children, posing a serious ingestion hazard. Consumers should stop using these lights immediately and contact Smfanlus for a full refund.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
This recall involves Qumeney Bicycle Light Sets, models BL-01 and BL-02. The lights are encased in silicone and come in six colors: black, white, red, green, yellow, and blue. They are sold in packs of six (one of each color) and 24 (four of each color). Each light contains two button cell batteries.

About 2,800 units were sold online at Amazon.com from March 2024 through February 2026, priced between $7 and $10 for the 6-pack and $15 to $18 for the 24-pack. The packaging has "QUMENEY" printed on the label at the top.

No injuries have been reported to date. The recall was announced on April 30, 2026, by the U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC).

hazards: The recalled bicycle lights violate the mandatory standard for consumer products with button cell and coin batteries because they contain button cell batteries that can be accessed easily by children, posing an ingestion hazard. Additionally, the bicycle lights do not have the warnings required under Reese's Law. When button cell or coin batteries are swallowed, the ingested batteries can cause serious injuries, internal chemical burns, and death.
remedies: Consumers should stop using the recalled bicycle lights immediately and contact Smfanlus for a full refund. Consumers will be asked to remove and properly dispose of the batteries and send a photo of the destroyed pieces of the light components to qumeneybicyclelightsrecall@outlook.com to receive a full refund. Note: Button cell batteries are hazardous. Batteries should be disposed of or recycled by following local hazardous waste procedures.
retailers: Online at Amazon.com from March 2024 through February 2026 for between $7 and $10 (6-pack) and between $15 and $18 (24-pack).
distributors: Xiamen Fanlu Technology Co., Ltd., doing business as Smfanlus, of China

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
Identify the recalled products by checking for the following details:

  • Product name: Qumeney Bicycle Light Sets
  • Models: BL-01 and BL-02
  • Packaging label with "QUMENEY" printed at the top
  • Silicone-encased bicycle lights available in black, white, red, green, yellow, and blue
  • Sold in packs of six (one of each color) or 24 (four of each color)
  • Each light contains two button cell batteries accessible through a compartment shown in recall images

content.subtitle: What Consumers Should Do
content.text:
Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled bicycle lights. To receive a full refund, contact Smfanlus by email at qumeneybicyclelightsrecall@outlook.com or online at their Amazon seller page and click "Recall" at the top.

Consumers will be asked to remove and properly dispose of the batteries following local hazardous waste guidelines. They must then send a photo of the destroyed light components to the provided email address to confirm disposal and obtain the refund.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: The recalled bicycle lights contain button cell batteries that can be easily accessed by children. If swallowed, these batteries can cause serious internal injuries such as chemical burns, and in severe cases, death. The products do not comply with mandatory safety standards requiring secure battery compartments and proper warnings, increasing the risk of accidental ingestion.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: Consumers who purchased Qumeney Bicycle Light Sets, models BL-01 and BL-02, from Amazon.com between March 2024 and February 2026 are affected by this recall. The products were sold in 6-packs and 24-packs containing multiple colors of lights.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Smfanlus by email at qumeneybicyclelightsrecall@outlook.com or online at https://www.amazon.com/sp?seller=A1PBQ44FT8BEDW and click "Recall" at the top of the page for more information.

faq.answer: They were sold online at Amazon.com from March 2024 through February 2026.

headline: Acer America Corporation Recalls AES015 Folding Electric Scooters Over Risk of Injury from Unexpected Folding

image.caption: Recalled AES015 Folding Electric Scooter

description: Acer recalls about 510 AES015 folding electric scooters sold 2023-2026 due to a loose screw causing fall hazards. Free repair tools available from Acer.
keywords: Acer AES015 Two-Wheeled Folding Electric Scooters
A loose screw can cause the front tube to fold down unexpectedly, posing a risk of injury from a fall hazard.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: Acer America Corporation is recalling about 510 AES015 two-wheeled folding electric scooters because a loose screw can cause the front tube to fold down unexpectedly, posing a fall hazard. Consumers should stop using the scooter immediately and contact Acer for free repair tools and instructions to tighten the screw.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
This recall involves the Acer AES015 two-wheeled folding electric scooters. These scooters fold by lowering the front tube down to the platform for compact transport and storage. They were sold in black with green and white decals on the front tube and platform sides, and the wheels feature a green circle around the rim. The model number AES015 is located on a label on the front tube.

About 510 units were sold in the United States, with an additional 311 sold in Canada and about 150 in Mexico. The scooters were sold online at Acer.com, Amazon.com, and in stores at BrandMart between June 2023 and February 2026, priced between $70 and $245.

No injuries have been reported in the United States. However, internationally there was one report of the front tube folding down unexpectedly, causing a fall and minor abrasions.

products.Name: Acer AES015 Two-Wheeled Folding Electric Scooters
products.NumberOfUnits: About 510 (In addition, about 311 were sold in Canada and about 150 were sold in Mexico)

hazards: A loose screw can cause the front tube to fold down unexpectedly, posing a risk of injury from a fall hazard.
remedies: Consumers should stop using the recalled folding electric scooter immediately and contact Acer at https://www.acer.com/us-en/support/alerts-recalls for instructions on how to tighten the screw. Free repair tools will be available to consumers upon request.
injuries: None reported in the United States (and one report of the tube folding down resulting in a fall and minor abrasions internationally).
retailers: Online at Acer.com, Amazon.com and in stores at BrandMart from between June 2023 and February 2026 for between $245 and $70.
importers: Acer America Corporation, of Irvine, California

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
To identify if your scooter is part of this recall, check the following:

  • The model number AES015, located on the label on the front tube of the scooter.
  • The scooter’s color: black with green and white decals on the front tube and platform sides.
  • The wheels have a distinctive green circle around the rim.

Refer to the label on the front tube to confirm the model number exactly as AES015.

content.subtitle: What Consumers Should Do
content.text:
If you own this scooter, stop using it immediately to avoid the risk of injury. Contact Acer America Corporation for instructions on how to tighten the loose screw that causes the hazard. Free repair tools will be provided upon request.

You can reach Acer by:

  • Calling toll-free at 866-514-2237, Monday through Friday, 6 a.m. to 3 p.m. ET
  • Emailing acer.esseries@acer.com
  • Visiting https://www.acer.com/us-en/support/alerts-recalls or www.acer.com and clicking on "Product Recalls" for more information.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: The hazard involves a loose screw on the folding mechanism of the scooter’s front tube. If this screw becomes loose, the front tube can fold down unexpectedly while the scooter is in use. This sudden folding can cause the rider to lose balance and fall, potentially resulting in injury. The risk is primarily from falls caused by the scooter unexpectedly folding during operation.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: This recall affects anyone who owns or uses an Acer AES015 two-wheeled folding electric scooter purchased between June 2023 and February 2026 from Acer.com, Amazon.com, or BrandMart stores. The scooter is identifiable by its black color with green and white decals and green circles on the wheel rims.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Acer America Corporation toll-free at 866-514-2237 from 6 a.m. to 3 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, email at acer.esseries@acer.com or online at https://www.acer.com/us-en/support/alerts-recalls or go to www.acer.com and click on "Product Recalls" for more information.

faq.answer: A loose screw in the folding mechanism can cause the front tube to fold down unexpectedly while riding, creating a fall hazard.

faq.question: How can I tell if my scooter is part of this recall?
faq.answer: Check the model number AES015 on the label located on the front tube. The scooter is black with green and white decals and has green circles around the wheel rims.

faq.question: What should I do if I have a recalled scooter?
faq.answer: Stop using the scooter immediately and contact Acer for free repair tools and instructions to tighten the screw to fix the hazard.

headline: DGIVOVO US Recalls Electric Pressure Washers Due to Risk of Shock and Electrocution

image.caption: Recalled DGIVOVO US Electric Pressure Washer

description: DGIVOVO US recalls electric pressure washers sold online due to shock and electrocution hazards from missing ground-fault circuit interrupter (GFCI).
keywords: The recalled pressure washers lack an integral ground-fault circuit-interrupter (GFCI), posing a serious risk of injury or death from shock and electrocution hazards.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: DGIVOVO US has recalled about 50 electric pressure washers sold online at Amazon.com between October 2025 and January 2026 because they lack a ground-fault circuit interrupter (GFCI), posing serious shock and electrocution risks. Consumers should stop using the product immediately and contact DGIVOVO US for a free replacement.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
This recall involves DGIVOVO US-branded electric pressure washers that come in red, green, or blue with black accents. The units measure approximately seven inches high, nine inches wide, and eight inches deep. Included accessories are a 500 ml spray can attachment, foam cannon, four connect nozzles, a 20-foot hose, and two rollers. The model number "C4012PLUS-1500C" is printed on a label located on the back of the pressure washers.

About 50 units were sold online at Amazon.com from October 2025 through January 2026 for around $90. The pressure washers were imported by Ningbopingyao E-Commerce Ltd., doing business as DGIVOVO US, based in China. No injuries have been reported to date.

The recall was announced on April 30, 2026, due to the pressure washers lacking an integral ground-fault circuit interrupter (GFCI), which creates a serious risk of shock and electrocution to users.

hazards: The recalled pressure washers lack an integral ground-fault circuit-interrupter (GFCI), posing a serious risk of injury or death from shock and electrocution hazards.
remedies: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled pressure washers and contact DGIVOVO US for a free replacement pressure washer of at least equal value to the recalled pressure washer, including shipping. Consumers will be asked to destroy the pressure washer by unplugging it and cutting the power cord in half and send a photo of the destroyed power washer to dgivovo@163.com. Consumers should then dispose of the destroyed recalled product.

headline: Earthtec 7 3/8-Inch Pool Drain Covers Recalled Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death

image.caption: Recalled Earthtec 7 3/8-inch Pool Drain Cover (front view)

description: Earthtec pool drain covers are recalled for violating safety standards, posing entrapment and drowning risks. About 211 units sold online at Amazon.com are affected.
keywords: The recalled drain covers violate the entrapment protection standards of the Virginia Graeme Baker Pool and Spa Safety Act (VGBA), posing entrapment and drowning hazards to swimmers and bathers.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: Earthtec 7 3/8-inch pool drain covers sold online at Amazon.com are recalled because they do not meet federal safety standards, creating a risk of entrapment and drowning. Consumers should stop using these drain covers immediately and contact Tonyidea for a refund.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
This recall involves Earthtec pool drain covers that measure 7 3/8 inches in diameter. The covers are white, made of ABS plastic, and come with two screws for installation. Approximately 211 units were sold online at Amazon.com from October 2025 through April 2026 for about $15 each.

The recalled drain covers violate the entrapment protection standards set by the Virginia Graeme Baker Pool and Spa Safety Act (VGBA). This violation creates a serious risk of entrapment and drowning hazards for swimmers and bathers. No injuries have been reported to date.

The manufacturer of the drain covers is Shenzhen Qiangonghui Technology Co., Ltd., doing business as Tonyidea, based in China. The recall was announced on April 30, 2026.

hazards: The recalled drain covers violate the entrapment protection standards of the Virginia Graeme Baker Pool and Spa Safety Act (VGBA), posing entrapment and drowning hazards to swimmers and bathers.
remedies: Pool owners, pool operators and consumers should stop using pools with the recalled pool drain covers immediately. To receive a refund, consumers should remove the drain cover from the pool, and destroy it, take a photo of the removed cover, and send it to Tonyidea by email at earthtecrecall@outlook.com. Ensure all pools and spas have VGBA-compliant drain covers and teach children to stay away from drains.
retailers: Online at Amazon.com from October 2025 through April 2026 for about $15.
manufacturers: Shenzhen Qiangonghui Technology Co., Ltd., dba Tonyidea

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
To identify the recalled product, look for the following details:

  • Drain cover diameter: 7 3/8 inches
  • Color: White
  • Material: ABS plastic
  • Includes two screws for installation
  • Sold online at Amazon.com from October 2025 through April 2026

Images of the recalled drain covers show the front, back, and top views for comparison. Check your pool drain covers against these characteristics to determine if your product is part of the recall.

content.subtitle: What Consumers Should Do
content.text:
If you have the recalled Earthtec pool drain covers, stop using the pool immediately to prevent any risk of entrapment or drowning. Remove the drain cover from your pool and destroy it to ensure it is not reused.

To receive a refund, take a photo of the removed drain cover and email it to Tonyidea at earthtecrecall@outlook.com. Make sure all your pools and spas have drain covers that comply with the Virginia Graeme Baker Pool and Spa Safety Act.

Additionally, teach children to stay away from pool drains and never leave them unattended near pools.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text:
The recalled Earthtec pool drain covers do not comply with federal safety standards designed to prevent swimmers from becoming trapped by suction at pool drains. Entrapment can occur when a drain cover allows a strong suction force, which can hold a swimmer underwater and lead to drowning. The Virginia Graeme Baker Pool and Spa Safety Act requires drain covers to meet specific entrapment protection standards to reduce this risk.

Using these non-compliant drain covers increases the risk of serious injury or death, especially for children and vulnerable swimmers. It is important to ensure all pool drain covers meet VGBA standards to maintain safe swimming environments.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: This recall affects pool owners, pool operators, and consumers who have purchased and installed the Earthtec 7 3/8-inch pool drain covers. The covers were sold online at Amazon.com between October 2025 and April 2026. If you have a swimming pool or spa with these drain covers, you should consider yourself affected by this recall.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Tonyidea by email at earthtecrecall@outlook.com.

faq.answer: The drain covers violate federal safety standards, posing entrapment and drowning hazards due to suction forces that can trap swimmers underwater.

faq.answer: Check if your drain cover is white, made of ABS plastic, measures 7 3/8 inches in diameter, and was purchased online at Amazon.com between October 2025 and April 2026.

headline: Recall of StrikeLock Hoistway Door Locking Device for Residential Elevators Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death

image.caption: Recalled StrikeLock Door Locking Device

description: Residential Elevators recalls about 450 StrikeLock hoistway door locking devices due to risk of elevator movement with door open causing fall hazards.
keywords: Residential Elevators StrikeLock Hoistway Door Locking Device
The elevator's StrikeLock hoistway door locking device can allow the elevator cab to move with a landing door open, posing a risk of serious injury or death due to fall and injury hazards.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: The StrikeLock hoistway door locking device used in some residential elevators can fail to secure the landing door properly, allowing the elevator cab to move with the door open. This poses a serious risk of falls and injury. About 450 devices sold from April 2024 through March 2026 are affected. Consumers should stop using elevators with this device and contact Residential Elevators or their local dealer for a free repair.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
This recall involves the StrikeLock hoistway door locking device, a silver-colored component installed in the landing door jamb of residential elevator hoistways. It resembles a standard door latch plate and is designed to secure the hoistway landing door.

About 450 of these devices were sold by Residential Elevators and authorized elevator dealers nationwide from April 2024 through March 2026, typically for around $1,000. The devices were manufactured in the United States and distributed by Residential Elevators, Inc., based in Crawfordville, Florida.

No injuries have been reported to date. The recall was announced on April 30, 2026, due to the risk that the StrikeLock device can allow the elevator cab to move while the landing door is open, creating serious fall and injury hazards.

products.Name: Residential Elevators StrikeLock Hoistway Door Locking Device

hazards: The elevator's StrikeLock hoistway door locking device can allow the elevator cab to move with a landing door open, posing a risk of serious injury or death due to fall and injury hazards.
remedies: Consumers should immediately stop using the residential elevator systems where StrikeLock hoistway door locking devices are installed and contact Residential Elevators or the local elevator dealer that installed their residential elevator system to schedule a free repair of their StrikeLock hoistway door locking devices. Consumers should contact Residential Elevators or the local elevator dealer that installed their residential elevator system if they are uncertain whether their residential elevator has a StrikeLock hoistway door locking device installed.
retailers: Residential Elevators and authorized elevator dealers nationwide from April 2024 through March 2026 for about $1,000.
distributors: Residential Elevators, Inc., of Crawfordville, Florida

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
Check the hoistway landing door jamb of your residential elevator for a silver-colored door locking device that looks similar to a standard door latch plate. This is the StrikeLock hoistway door locking device subject to recall.

If you are unsure whether your elevator has this device installed, contact Residential Elevators or the local elevator dealer that installed your system for assistance.

content.subtitle: What Consumers Should Do
content.text:
Consumers should immediately stop using residential elevators equipped with the StrikeLock hoistway door locking device.

  • Contact Residential Elevators toll-free at 800-832-2004, Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET.
  • Email StrikeLock@Residentialelevators.com for more information.
  • Visit www.residentialelevators.com/recall-information/strikelock-locking-device or www.residentialelevators.com and click on "Recall Information" at the bottom of the page.
  • Schedule a free repair of the StrikeLock device through Residential Elevators or your local elevator dealer.

If you are uncertain whether your elevator has the StrikeLock device, contact Residential Elevators or your local dealer for verification.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: The hazard arises because the StrikeLock hoistway door locking device may fail to keep the elevator landing door securely locked. If the door is open while the elevator cab moves, a person could fall down the elevator shaft or be injured by the moving elevator. This risk of serious injury or death is why the device is being recalled.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text:
Consumers who have residential elevators installed with the StrikeLock hoistway door locking device are affected by this recall. These devices were sold and installed by Residential Elevators and authorized dealers nationwide between April 2024 and March 2026.

If you have a residential elevator installed during this period, especially if purchased from Residential Elevators or an authorized dealer, you should verify whether your elevator uses the StrikeLock device.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Residential Elevators toll-free at 800-832-2004 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, email at StrikeLock@Residentialelevators.com, or online at www.residentialelevators.com/recall-information/strikelock-locking-device or www.residentialelevators.com and click on "Recall Information" at the bottom of the page for more information.

faq.answer: Because it can allow the elevator cab to move while the landing door is open, posing a risk of serious injury or death from falls and other hazards.

faq.answer: About 450 StrikeLock hoistway door locking devices sold nationwide from April 2024 through March 2026 are affected.

title: Residential Elevators Recall: StrikeLock Hoistway Door Locking Device Due to Fall and Injury Hazards

editorialBrief.title: Residential Elevators Recall: StrikeLock Hoistway Door Locking Device Due to Fall and Injury Hazards
editorialBrief.headline: Recall of StrikeLock Hoistway Door Locking Device for Residential Elevators Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death
editorialBrief.metaDescription: Residential Elevators recalls about 450 StrikeLock hoistway door locking devices due to risk of elevator movement with door open causing fall hazards.
editorialBrief.quickAnswerHtml: The StrikeLock hoistway door locking device used in some residential elevators can fail to secure the landing door properly, allowing the elevator cab to move with the door open. This poses a serious risk of falls and injury. About 450 devices sold from April 2024 through March 2026 are affected. Consumers should stop using elevators with this device and contact Residential Elevators or their local dealer for a free repair.
editorialBrief.recallSummaryHtml:
This recall involves the StrikeLock hoistway door locking device, a silver-colored component installed in the landing door jamb of residential elevator hoistways. It resembles a standard door latch plate and is designed to secure the hoistway landing door.

About 450 of these devices were sold by Residential Elevators and authorized elevator dealers nationwide from April 2024 through March 2026, typically for around $1,000. The devices were manufactured in the United States and distributed by Residential Elevators, Inc., based in Crawfordville, Florida.

No injuries have been reported to date. The recall was announced on April 30, 2026, due to the risk that the StrikeLock device can allow the elevator cab to move while the landing door is open, creating serious fall and injury hazards.
editorialBrief.riskOverviewHtml: The hazard arises because the StrikeLock hoistway door locking device may fail to keep the elevator landing door securely locked. If the door is open while the elevator cab moves, a person could fall down the elevator shaft or be injured by the moving elevator. This risk of serious injury or death is why the device is being recalled.
editorialBrief.whoIsAffectedHtml:
Consumers who have residential elevators installed with the StrikeLock hoistway door locking device are affected by this recall. These devices were sold and installed by Residential Elevators and authorized dealers nationwide between April 2024 and March 2026.

If you have a residential elevator installed during this period, especially if purchased from Residential Elevators or an authorized dealer, you should verify whether your elevator uses the StrikeLock device.
editorialBrief.howToIdentifyProductHtml:
Check the hoistway landing door jamb of your residential elevator for a silver-colored door locking device that looks similar to a standard door latch plate. This is the StrikeLock hoistway door locking device subject to recall.

If you are unsure whether your elevator has this device installed, contact Residential Elevators or the local elevator dealer that installed your system for assistance.
editorialBrief.whatToDoHtml:
Consumers should immediately stop using residential elevators equipped with the StrikeLock hoistway door locking device.

  • Contact Residential Elevators toll-free at 800-832-2004, Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET.
  • Email StrikeLock@Residentialelevators.com for more information.
  • Visit www.residentialelevators.com/recall-information/strikelock-locking-device or www.residentialelevators.com and click on "Recall Information" at the bottom of the page.
  • Schedule a free repair of the StrikeLock device through Residential Elevators or your local elevator dealer.

If you are uncertain whether your elevator has the StrikeLock device, contact Residential Elevators or your local dealer for verification.
editorialBrief.sourceTransparencyHtml: Recalls Atlas has summarized this recall based on the official U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) recall notice. The original source data and links are preserved separately to ensure transparency and accuracy.

headline: Hepo Adult Portable Bed Rails Recalled for Risk of Entrapment, Asphyxiation, and Injury

image.caption: Recalled Hepo Adult Portable Bed Rail

description: Hepo adult portable bed rails are recalled due to entrapment and asphyxiation risks. Consumers should stop use and contact Hepo Care Medical Equipment Online for a.
keywords: The recalled bed rails violate the mandatory standard for adult portable bed rails because users can become entrapped within the bed rail or between the bed rail and the side of the mattress, posing a serious entrapment hazard and risk of death by asphyxiation. The bed rails also do not meet structural stability or retention strap requirements, posing a fall hazard. The bed rails' push pins and push pin holes are incorrectly sized, posing a laceration hazard. In addition, the bed rails do not bear the required hazard warning labels.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: The Hepo-branded adult portable bed rails, model 110039, have been recalled because they pose serious risks of entrapment, asphyxiation, falls, and lacerations. Consumers should stop using these bed rails immediately and contact Hepo Care Medical Equipment Online for a full refund and instructions on safe disposal.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
This recall involves Hepo Adult Portable Bed Rails, model 110039, imported and sold by Hepo Care Medical Equipment Online. The bed rails feature white tubing with black foam rubber handle grips, a black fabric pouch, and measure approximately 16 inches wide by 25 inches high. The model number is found on the original product packaging.

About 2,200 units were sold online at Walmart.com from November 2025 through January 2026 for about $100, and on Amazon.com from June 2025 through January 2026 for between $35 and $40. The product was manufactured by Mobility Source Medical Technology Co., Ltd. in China and imported by Lingqingxiang Health Technology Co., Ltd., doing business as Hepo Care Medical Equipment Online Ltd.

No injuries have been reported to date related to this recall.

hazards: The recalled bed rails violate the mandatory standard for adult portable bed rails because users can become entrapped within the bed rail or between the bed rail and the side of the mattress, posing a serious entrapment hazard and risk of death by asphyxiation. The bed rails also do not meet structural stability or retention strap requirements, posing a fall hazard. The bed rails' push pins and push pin holes are incorrectly sized, posing a laceration hazard. In addition, the bed rails do not bear the required hazard warning labels.
remedies: Consumers should stop using the recalled adult portable bed rails immediately and contact Hepo Care Medical Equipment Online for a full refund. Consumers will be asked to destroy the bed rails by dismantling the bed rails, cutting off the storage bag and writing "RECALLED" on the upper and lower rails with permanent marker, take a photo of the destroyed rails and upload the photo to www.hepolife.com/bed-rail-safety-recall or email the photo to manager@lqxmedical.com.
retailers: Online at Walmart.com from November 2025 through January 2026 for about $100 Amazon.com from June 2025 through January 2026 for between $35 and $40.
importers: Lingqingxiangjiankangkejigufenyouxiangongsi (Lingqingxiang Health Technology Co., Ltd.) dba Hepo Care Medical Equipment Online Ltd., of China
manufacturers: Mobility Source Medical Technology Co., Ltd. of China

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
Consumers should check for the following identifying features to determine if they have a recalled product:

  • Brand: Hepo
  • Product: Adult Portable Bed Rails
  • Model number: 110039 (found on the original product packaging)
  • Appearance: White tubing with black foam rubber handle grips and a black fabric pouch
  • Dimensions: Approximately 16 inches wide by 25 inches high

Refer to the original packaging and product labels to confirm the model number and brand.

content.subtitle: What Consumers Should Do
content.text:
Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled bed rails to avoid risk of injury or death. To receive a full refund, contact Hepo Care Medical Equipment Online by email at manager@lqxmedical.com or visit https://www.hepolife.com/bed-rail-safety-recall. You can also visit https://www.hepolife.com and click "Recall" at the top of the page for more information.

Consumers will be asked to dismantle the bed rails, cut off the storage bag, and mark "RECALLED" on the upper and lower rails with a permanent marker. Then, take a photo of the destroyed bed rails and upload it to the recall website or email it to manager@lqxmedical.com to complete the refund process.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: The recalled bed rails violate mandatory safety standards for adult portable bed rails. They pose a serious entrapment hazard because users can become trapped within the bed rail or between the bed rail and the mattress, which can lead to asphyxiation and death. Additionally, the bed rails do not meet structural stability or retention strap requirements, increasing the risk of falls. The push pins and push pin holes are incorrectly sized, creating a risk of cuts or lacerations. Furthermore, the bed rails lack the required hazard warning labels, which are essential for safe use.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: This recall affects consumers who purchased the Hepo Adult Portable Bed Rails, model 110039, from Walmart.com or Amazon.com between June 2025 and January 2026. These bed rails were sold online and are intended for adult use to provide support and safety while in bed.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Hepo Care Medical Equipment Online by email at manager@lqxmedical.com or online at https://www.hepolife.com/bed-rail-safety-recall or https://www.hepolife.com and click "Recall" at the top of the page for more information.

faq.question: What specific hazard do the recalled Hepo adult portable bed rails pose?
faq.answer: The bed rails pose a serious risk of entrapment and asphyxiation because users can become trapped within the bed rail or between the bed rail and mattress. They also present fall and laceration hazards due to structural and design defects.

faq.question: How can I tell if my bed rails are part of this recall?
faq.answer: Check if your bed rails are Hepo brand, model number 110039, with white tubing, black foam rubber handle grips, and a black fabric pouch. The model number is on the original packaging, and the rails measure about 16 inches wide by 25 inches high.

faq.question: What should I do if I have the recalled bed rails?
faq.answer: Stop using the bed rails immediately. Contact Hepo Care Medical Equipment Online for a full refund and follow their instructions to dismantle and mark the rails as recalled, then submit a photo of the destroyed rails to complete the refund.

headline: UHOMEPRO Recalls 5-Drawer Dressers Due to Serious Injury and Death Risks from Tip-Over

image.caption: Recalled UHOMEPRO 5-Drawer Dresser (front)

description: UHOMEPRO recalls 5-drawer dressers sold online at Walmart.com due to tip-over and entrapment hazards posing serious injury risks to children.
keywords: The recalled dressers are unstable if they are not anchored to the wall, posing tip-over and entrapment hazards that can result in risks of serious injuries or death to children. The dressers violate the mandatory safety standard as required by the STURDY Act.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: The UHOMEPRO 5-Drawer Dressers sold online at Walmart.com between November 2025 and January 2026 are recalled because they can tip over if not anchored, posing serious injury or death risks to children. Consumers should stop using these dressers immediately if unanchored and contact UHOMEPRO for a full refund and disposal instructions.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
This recall involves UHOMEPRO 5-Drawer Dressers made of particle board, available in black and white finishes. Each dresser measures 26 inches long by 15.7 inches wide by 38.6 inches tall and weighs about 66 pounds. The dresser features five drawers, each equipped with a golden handle.

About 5,190 units were sold online at Walmart.com from November 2025 through January 2026 for approximately $100. The dressers were imported by UHOMEPRO Inc., based in China.

The dressers are being recalled because they are unstable if not anchored to a wall, creating tip-over and entrapment hazards. These hazards can result in serious injuries or death, particularly to children. No injuries have been reported to date. The dressers also violate the mandatory safety standard required by the STURDY Act for clothing storage units.

hazards: The recalled dressers are unstable if they are not anchored to the wall, posing tip-over and entrapment hazards that can result in risks of serious injuries or death to children. The dressers violate the mandatory safety standard as required by the STURDY Act.
remedies: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled dressers if they are not anchored to the wall and place them in an area that children cannot access. Contact UHOMEPRO for instructions on how to identify affected units and dispose of the dressers to receive a full refund. Consumers will be asked to write the word "recalled" on the top and all sides of the dresser with a permanent marker and to submit a photo to uhomeprorecall@outlook.com demonstrating disposal of the product.
retailers: Online at Walmart.com from November 2025 through January 2026 for about $100.

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
Consumers should look for the following identifying features to determine if their dresser is part of the recall:

  • Product: UHOMEPRO 5-Drawer Dresser
  • Material: Particle board
  • Colors: Black or white finish
  • Dimensions: Approximately 26 inches long, 15.7 inches wide, and 38.6 inches tall
  • Weight: About 66 pounds
  • Design: Five drawers, each with a golden handle

Photos of the dresser’s front, side, and back views are available from the CPSC recall notice for visual comparison. The dressers were sold exclusively online at Walmart.com during the specified timeframe.

content.subtitle: What Consumers Should Do
content.text:
If you own one of these dressers and it is not anchored to a wall, stop using it immediately and move it to a location where children cannot access it. Contact UHOMEPRO by email at uhomeprorecall@outlook.com to receive instructions on how to identify your specific unit and how to dispose of it safely.

Consumers will be asked to write the word "recalled" on the top and all sides of the dresser using a permanent marker and submit a photo showing the dresser marked and disposed of to the provided email address. UHOMEPRO will provide a full refund upon verification.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: The main hazard with these dressers is their instability when not anchored to a wall. This instability can cause the dresser to tip over, which poses a serious risk of injury or death, especially to young children who might climb on or pull the drawers. Additionally, tip-over incidents can lead to entrapment hazards where children may become trapped underneath or behind the dresser. The recalled dressers do not meet the safety requirements mandated by the STURDY Act, which aims to prevent such tip-over accidents.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: This recall affects consumers who purchased the UHOMEPRO 5-Drawer Dressers online at Walmart.com between November 2025 and January 2026. Families with young children should be particularly cautious, as children are most vulnerable to tip-over and entrapment hazards posed by these dressers.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: UHOMEPRO by email at uhomeprorecall@outlook.com.

headline: Malker Bicycle Light Sets Recalled Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Button Cell Battery Ingestion

image.caption: Recalled Malker Bicycle Light Sets in Eight-Pack

description: Malker Bicycle Light Sets sold on Amazon are recalled due to accessible button cell batteries posing a serious ingestion hazard to children.
keywords: The recalled bicycle lights violate the mandatory standard for consumer products with button cell and coin batteries because they contain button cell batteries that can be accessed easily by children, posing an ingestion hazard. Additionally, the bicycle lights do not have the warnings required under Reese's Law. When button cell or coin batteries are swallowed, the ingested batteries can cause serious injuries, internal chemical burns and death.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: Malker Bicycle Light Sets sold on Amazon between October and November 2025 are recalled because their button cell batteries can be easily accessed by children, posing a serious ingestion hazard. Consumers should stop using these lights immediately and contact MalkerDirect for a full refund.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
This recall involves Malker Bicycle Light Sets sold in 4- or 8-pack sizes. The lights come in red, black, or white silicone covers, emitting red or white light depending on the color. Each light contains two preinstalled CR2032 button cell batteries. The sets have "Malker Bicycle Light" printed on the front packaging label.

About 7,000 units were sold online at Amazon.com from October 2025 through November 2025, priced between $7 and $10. The products were distributed by Shenzhen Maikeer Industrial Co., Ltd., doing business as MalkerDirect, based in China.

No injuries have been reported to date.

hazards: The recalled bicycle lights violate the mandatory standard for consumer products with button cell and coin batteries because they contain button cell batteries that can be accessed easily by children, posing an ingestion hazard. Additionally, the bicycle lights do not have the warnings required under Reese's Law. When button cell or coin batteries are swallowed, the ingested batteries can cause serious injuries, internal chemical burns and death.
remedies: Consumers should stop using the recalled bicycle lights immediately and contact MalkerDirect for a full refund. Consumers should throw the bicycle lights away and send a photo of the product in the trash to USMalkerCustomerservice@outlook.com. Note: Button cell batteries are hazardous. Batteries should be disposed of or recycled by following local hazardous waste procedures.
retailers: Online at Amazon.com from October 2025 through November 2025 for between $7 and $10.
distributors: Shenzhen Maikeer Industrial Co., Ltd., doing business as MalkerDirect, of China

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
Identify the recalled products by checking for the following details:

  • Product name: "Malker Bicycle Light Sets"
  • Packaging label with "Malker Bicycle Light" printed on the front
  • Sold in 4- or 8-pack sizes with combinations of red, black, or white silicone lights
  • Each light contains two preinstalled CR2032 button cell batteries
  • Purchased from Amazon.com between October and November 2025

content.subtitle: What Consumers Should Do
content.text: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled bicycle lights. They should throw the lights away following local hazardous waste disposal or recycling procedures for button cell batteries. To receive a full refund, consumers must send a photo of the product in the trash to MalkerDirect at USMalkerCustomerservice@outlook.com.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: The recalled bicycle lights contain button cell batteries that can be easily accessed by children. If swallowed, these batteries can cause serious internal chemical burns, injuries, or even death. The lights do not comply with mandatory safety standards requiring secure battery compartments and appropriate warnings, increasing the risk of battery ingestion.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: Consumers who purchased Malker Bicycle Light Sets in 4- or 8-pack sizes from Amazon.com between October and November 2025 are affected by this recall. These products were sold under the MalkerDirect brand and contain accessible CR2032 button cell batteries.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: MalkerDirect by email at USMalkerCustomerservice@outlook.com.

faq.answer: The hazard is that the button cell batteries inside the lights can be easily accessed and swallowed by children, which can cause serious injuries, chemical burns, or death.

faq.answer: They were sold online at Amazon.com from October 2025 through November 2025.

faq.question: How can I get a refund for the recalled bicycle lights?
faq.answer: Consumers should stop using the lights, dispose of them safely, and send a photo of the product in the trash to USMalkerCustomerservice@outlook.com to receive a full refund from MalkerDirect.

headline: Segmart 4-Drawer Dressers Recalled Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Tip-Over and Entrapment

image.caption: Recalled Black Segmart 4-Drawer Dresser (front)

description: Segmart 4-Drawer Dressers sold on Walmart.com are recalled for tip-over hazards posing serious injury or death risks. Consumers should stop use and contact SOFT for a.
keywords: The recalled dressers are unstable if they are not anchored to the wall, posing tip-over and entrapment hazards that can result in risks of serious injuries or death to children. The dressers violate the mandatory safety standard as required by the STURDY Act.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: The Segmart 4-Drawer Dressers sold online at Walmart.com between January 2025 and March 2026 are recalled because they can tip over if not anchored, risking serious injury or death, especially to children. Consumers should stop using these dressers if unanchored, keep children away, and contact SOFT for a full refund and disposal instructions.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
This recall involves Segmart 4-Drawer Dressers made of particle board, available in black and white colors. The dressers measure 15.7 inches long by 26 inches wide by 28.5 inches tall and weigh approximately 70 pounds. Model numbers "FFLWM002BK" (black) and "FFLWM002WE" (white) are printed on labels located on the product packaging.

About 16,440 units were sold online at Walmart.com from January 2025 through March 2026 for about $80 each. The dressers were distributed by SOFT INC., based in Denver, Colorado, and manufactured in China and the United Arab Emirates.

No injuries have been reported to date. The recall was announced on April 30, 2026, due to tip-over and entrapment hazards that violate mandatory safety standards required by the STURDY Act.

hazards: The recalled dressers are unstable if they are not anchored to the wall, posing tip-over and entrapment hazards that can result in risks of serious injuries or death to children. The dressers violate the mandatory safety standard as required by the STURDY Act.
remedies: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled dressers if they are not anchored to the wall and place them in an area that children cannot access. Contact SOFT for instructions on how to identify affected units and dispose of the dressers to receive a full refund. Consumers will be asked to submit a photo to segmartdresserecall@outlook.com demonstrating disposal of the product.
retailers: Online at Walmart from January 2025 through March 2026 for about $80.

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
To identify if you have a recalled Segmart 4-Drawer Dresser, check the following:

  • The dresser is made of particle board and has four drawers.
  • The dresser measures approximately 15.7 inches long by 26 inches wide by 28.5 inches tall and weighs about 70 pounds.
  • The dresser color is either black or white.
  • Look for the model number on the product packaging label: "FFLWM002BK" for black dressers or "FFLWM002WE" for white dressers.

Images of the dresser front, side, and back, as well as the packaging label with model numbers, can help confirm identification.

content.subtitle: What Consumers Should Do
content.text:
If you own a recalled Segmart 4-Drawer Dresser, immediately stop using it if it is not anchored to the wall. Move the dresser to a location where children cannot access it to prevent tip-over and entrapment risks.

Contact SOFT by email at segmartdresserecall@outlook.com or visit Segmart.com/recall-dresser or Segmart.com and click "Recall" at the top of the page for detailed instructions on identifying affected units and how to dispose of the dresser safely.

Consumers will be asked to submit a photo demonstrating disposal of the product to receive a full refund.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text:
The recalled Segmart 4-Drawer Dressers are unstable if not properly anchored to a wall. This instability can cause the dresser to tip over, posing a serious risk of injury or death, particularly to children who may climb on or pull the dresser. Additionally, the dresser design creates entrapment hazards where children could become trapped or pinned.

The dresser fails to meet the mandatory safety requirements for clothing storage units, which are designed to prevent tip-over incidents. The risk is significant because tip-overs can cause crushing injuries or suffocation.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text:
Consumers who purchased Segmart 4-Drawer Dressers with model numbers "FFLWM002BK" (black) or "FFLWM002WE" (white) from Walmart.com between January 2025 and March 2026 are affected by this recall. These dressers were sold for about $80 and weigh approximately 70 pounds.

Households with children are especially at risk due to the tip-over and entrapment hazards associated with these dressers if they are not anchored to a wall.

headline: Recall of 1-K Kerosene Heater Fluid Portable Fuel Containers for Safety Violations and Fire Hazard

image.caption: Recalled 1-K Kerosene Heater Fluid Portable Fuel Container (quart)

description: Alliance Chemical recalls about 30,155 1-K Kerosene Heater Fluid containers due to flash fire and child poisoning risks from non-compliant fuel containers.
keywords: 1-K Kerosene Heater Fluid Portable Fuel Containers

The pre-filled fuel containers violate the mandatory safety standards for portable fuel containers because they lack flame mitigation devices required under the Portable Fuel Container Safety Act, posing a deadly risk of flash fire. In addition, the Children's Gasoline Burn Prevention Act requires all closures on portable kerosene fuel containers to be child-resistant. The container is not child-resistant, posing a risk of burn and poisoning to children.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: Alliance Chemical is recalling approximately 30,155 containers of 1-K Kerosene Heater Fluid because the fuel containers lack required safety features, posing risks of flash fire, burns, and child poisoning. Consumers should stop using these products immediately and contact Alliance Chemical for a full refund.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
This recall involves 1-K Kerosene Heater Fluid portable fuel containers sold by Alliance Chemical of Taylor, Texas. The containers are transparent plastic bottles with white caps and a multicolor label featuring "KEROSENE" and "1-K HEATER FUEL" in white capital letters on a single front panel. The label also includes warnings and distributor information.

About 30,155 units were sold online at Amazon.com and AllianceChemical.com from January 2023 through March 2026, with prices ranging from $18 to $102 depending on container size. No injuries have been reported to date.

The recall was announced on April 30, 2026, due to violations of mandatory safety standards for portable fuel containers, including the absence of flame mitigation devices and child-resistant closures.

products.Name: 1-K Kerosene Heater Fluid Portable Fuel Containers

hazards: The pre-filled fuel containers violate the mandatory safety standards for portable fuel containers because they lack flame mitigation devices required under the Portable Fuel Container Safety Act, posing a deadly risk of flash fire. In addition, the Children's Gasoline Burn Prevention Act requires all closures on portable kerosene fuel containers to be child-resistant. The container is not child-resistant, posing a risk of burn and poisoning to children.
remedies: Consumers should stop using the recalled fuel containers immediately, place them out of reach of children and contact Alliance Chemical for a full refund. Consumers will be asked to provide a dated photo of the product and dispose of the fuel following local hazardous waste procedures. Note: Kerosene fuel is hazardous. Fuel containers should be disposed of or recycled by following local hazardous waste procedures.
retailers: Online at Amazon.com and AllianceChemical.com from January 2023 through March 2026 for between $18 and $102 depending on the size.

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text: To identify the recalled product, look for transparent plastic containers with white caps labeled with a multicolor front panel. The label prominently displays "KEROSENE" and "1-K HEATER FUEL" in all capital white letters on a single front panel. The containers come in various sizes, including quart and gallon bottles. Images of the recalled containers show the label design and packaging for comparison.

content.subtitle: What Consumers Should Do
content.text:
Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled 1-K Kerosene Heater Fluid containers and place them out of reach of children. Contact Alliance Chemical for a full refund by calling toll-free at 512-365-6838 between 8 a.m. and 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, emailing sales@alliancechemical.com, or visiting alliancechemical.com/pages/recall and clicking "Recall" at the top of the page.

Consumers will be asked to provide a dated photo of the product. Dispose of the fuel following local hazardous waste procedures, as kerosene is hazardous. Do not attempt to reuse or refill the containers.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: The recalled 1-K Kerosene Heater Fluid containers pose serious safety risks because they do not meet federal safety standards. Specifically, they lack flame mitigation devices required to prevent flash fires, which can cause severe burns or death. Additionally, the containers are not child-resistant, increasing the risk of burns and poisoning if children access the fuel. Kerosene is hazardous and can cause serious harm if mishandled.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: This recall affects consumers who purchased 1-K Kerosene Heater Fluid portable fuel containers online from Amazon.com or AllianceChemical.com between January 2023 and March 2026. If you have one of these containers in your possession, you are urged to stop using it immediately due to the safety risks.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Alliance Chemical toll-free at 512-365-6838 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT Monday through Friday, email at sales@alliancechemical.com or online at alliancechemical.com/pages/recall or alliancechemical.com and click "Recall" at the top of the page for more information.

faq.question: What specific safety standards do the recalled containers violate?
faq.answer: The containers violate the Portable Fuel Container Safety Act by lacking flame mitigation devices and the Children's Gasoline Burn Prevention Act by not having child-resistant closures.

faq.question: Where were the recalled 1-K Kerosene Heater Fluid containers sold?
faq.answer: They were sold online at Amazon.com and AllianceChemical.com from January 2023 through March 2026.

faq.question: Are there any reported injuries related to this recall?
faq.answer: No injuries have been reported in connection with this recall.
