headline: Thermos Recalls Stainless King Food Jars and Sportsman Bottles Over Stopper Ejection Injuries

image.caption: Recalled Thermos 16-oz Stainless King Food Jar (SK3000), 40-oz Sportsman Food & Beverage Bottle (SK3010), and 24-oz Stainless King Food Jar (SK3020)

description: Thermos recalls 8.2 million Stainless King Food Jars and Sportsman Bottles due to stopper ejection causing impact and laceration injuries. Free replacements available.
keywords: Thermos Stainless King 3000 and 3020 Food Jars and Thermos Sportsman 3010 Food & Beverage Bottles

If perishable food or beverages are stored in the container for an extended period of time, the stopper can forcefully eject when opened, which can result in serious impact injury and laceration hazards to the consumer.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: Thermos is recalling about 8.2 million Stainless King Food Jars and Sportsman Food & Beverage Bottles because their stoppers can eject forcefully when opened, posing serious injury risks. Consumers should stop using these products immediately and contact Thermos for a free replacement stopper or bottle.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
This recall involves Thermos Stainless King Food Jars with model numbers SK3000 (16-oz) and SK3020 (24-oz), manufactured before July 2023, and all Thermos Sportsman Food & Beverage Bottles with model number SK3010 (40-oz). The recalled containers were sold in various colors and feature the Thermos trademark on the side, with model numbers printed on the bottom.

About 5.8 million Stainless King Food Jars and 2.3 million Sportsman Food & Beverage Bottles are included in this recall. These products were sold nationwide at retailers including Target, Walmart, and online at Amazon.com, Walmart.com, Target.com, and Thermos.com from approximately March 2008 through July 2024 for around $30.

Thermos has received 27 reports of the stopper forcefully ejecting upon opening, resulting in impact and laceration injuries requiring medical attention. Notably, three consumers suffered permanent vision loss after being struck in the eye.

products.Name: Thermos Stainless King 3000 and 3020 Food Jars and Thermos Sportsman 3010 Food & Beverage Bottles
products.NumberOfUnits: About 5.8 million Stainless King Food Jars and about 2.3 million Sportsman Food & Beverage Bottles

hazards: If perishable food or beverages are stored in the container for an extended period of time, the stopper can forcefully eject when opened, which can result in serious impact injury and laceration hazards to the consumer.
remedies: Consumers should stop using the recalled Food Jars and Bottles immediately and contact Thermos to receive a free replacement pressure relief stopper or replacement Bottle, depending on the model. For recalled 3000 and 3020 Food Jars, consumers will be asked to throw away the stopper and send a photo of the disposed stopper to Thermos. For recalled 3010 Bottles, consumers will be asked to return their recalled Bottle to Thermos using a prepaid shipping label.
injuries: Thermos has received 27 reports of consumers who were struck by a stopper that forcefully ejected from these containers upon opening, including complaints of impact and laceration injuries requiring medical attention. Three consumers suffered permanent vision loss after being struck in the eye.
retailers: Target, Walmart and other stores nationwide and online at Amazon.com, Walmart.com, Target.com and Thermos.com between around March 2008 and July 2024 for about $30.

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
To identify if your Thermos product is part of this recall, check the following:

  • Look for the Thermos trademark on the side of the container.
  • Find the model number printed on the bottom of the product. The recalled models are SK3000 (16-oz Stainless King Food Jar), SK3020 (24-oz Stainless King Food Jar), and SK3010 (40-oz Sportsman Food & Beverage Bottle).
  • Verify the manufacturing date is before July 2023 for SK3000 and SK3020 models.
  • Inspect the stopper: recalled products have a stopper without a pressure relief hole in the center.

Images of the recalled products and stopper are available in the official recall notice for comparison.

content.subtitle: What Consumers Should Do
content.text:
If you own a recalled Thermos Food Jar or Bottle, stop using it immediately to avoid injury. Follow these steps:

  • For SK3000 and SK3020 Food Jars: Throw away the stopper and take a photo of the disposed stopper. Contact Thermos to receive a free replacement pressure relief stopper.
  • For SK3010 Sportsman Bottles: Contact Thermos to receive a prepaid shipping label and return the recalled bottle. You will receive a replacement bottle.

Contact Thermos online at https://support.thermos.com or visit https://www.thermos.com and click "Contact Us" or "Recall Info." You can also call 662-563-6822 from 7 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. CT Monday through Friday.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text:
The recalled Thermos Food Jars and Bottles have stoppers without a pressure relief feature in the center. When perishable food or beverages are stored for extended periods, pressure can build inside the container. Upon opening, the stopper can eject forcefully, posing a risk of serious impact injuries and cuts to the user.

This hazard can cause injuries such as lacerations and eye trauma, including permanent vision loss in severe cases. The risk is significant enough that consumers should stop using these products immediately.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text:
This recall affects anyone who owns or uses Thermos Stainless King Food Jars model SK3000 or SK3020 manufactured before July 2023, and all Thermos Sportsman Food & Beverage Bottles model SK3010. These products were sold nationwide through major retailers and online platforms.

If you have one of these models, especially if you store perishable food or beverages in them for long periods, you are at risk of injury from the stopper ejecting unexpectedly.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Thermos online at https://support.thermos.com or go to https://www.thermos.com and click "Contact Us" or "Recall Info," or call 662-563-6822 from 7 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. CT Monday through Friday.

faq.question: What models of Thermos are included in this recall?
faq.answer: The recall includes Thermos Stainless King Food Jars model SK3000 (16-oz) and SK3020 (24-oz) manufactured before July 2023, and all Thermos Sportsman Food & Beverage Bottles model SK3010 (40-oz).

faq.question: What is the hazard associated with these Thermos products?
faq.answer: The stopper can eject forcefully when opened if pressure builds inside the container, causing serious impact injuries and lacerations, including permanent vision loss in some cases.

faq.question: How can I get a replacement for my recalled Thermos product?
faq.answer: Consumers should contact Thermos to receive a free replacement stopper for SK3000 and SK3020 Food Jars or return the SK3010 Sportsman Bottle using a prepaid shipping label to get a replacement bottle.

headline: KMUYSL Big Red Barn Farm Animal Playsets Recalled Due to Choking Hazard from Detachable Fence Handles

image.caption: Recalled KMUYSL-Big Red Barn Farm Animal Playset

description: KMUYSL Big Red Barn Farm Animal Playsets are recalled for choking hazards due to detachable fence handles, sold on Amazon by MISSJUNE from 2023 to 2025.
keywords: KMUYSL Big Red Barn Farm Animal Playsets

The recalled children's toys violate the small parts ban because the toy is intended for children under three years of age and the fence handles can detach from the toy, posing a deadly choking hazard.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: The KMUYSL Big Red Barn Farm Animal Playsets have been recalled because detachable fence handles pose a choking hazard to children under three years old. Consumers should stop using the toy immediately and contact MISSJUNE for a full refund.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
This recall involves KMUYSL Big Red Barn Farm Animal Playsets, which include a red barn, one farmer figure, and nine animal figures such as a cow, donkey, duck, sheep, chicken, goose, horse, pig, and rabbit. The barn measures approximately 9.6 inches long by 4.4 inches wide by 8.8 inches high, and the animal figures are about 1.8 inches long by 2 inches wide.

The recalled playsets violate the small parts ban because the fence handles can detach, creating a choking hazard for children under three years old. About 3,000 units were sold online on Amazon.com from July 2023 through June 2025 for around $20. No injuries have been reported to date.

The product was sold by Shenzhen Leye E-commerce Co., Ltd., doing business as MISSJUNE, and was manufactured in China. The model number "SY-MZ7S-TVMV" is printed on the label on the back of the packaging.

products.Name: KMUYSL Big Red Barn Farm Animal Playsets

hazards: The recalled children's toys violate the small parts ban because the toy is intended for children under three years of age and the fence handles can detach from the toy, posing a deadly choking hazard.
remedies: Consumers should stop using the recalled toys immediately, take them away from children and contact MISSJUNE for a full refund. Consumers will be asked to remove and properly dispose of the fence handles into the trash and send a photo of the disposed product to kmuyslfarmanimalrecall@gmail.com to receive a full refund.
retailers: Online at Amazon.com from July 2023 through June 2025 for about $20.
distributors: Shenzhen Leye E-commerce Co., Ltd., dba MISSJUNE, of China

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
Consumers should look for the KMUYSL Big Red Barn Farm Animal Playsets, which include a red barn, one farmer figure, and nine animal figures (cow, donkey, duck, sheep, chicken, goose, horse, pig, rabbit). The barn measures about 9.6 inches long by 4.4 inches wide by 8.8 inches high. The animal figures are approximately 1.8 inches long by 2 inches wide.

The model number "SY-MZ7S-TVMV" is printed on the label located on the back of the packaging. Photos of the recalled product and the detachable fence handles are available in the official recall notice.

content.subtitle: What Consumers Should Do
content.text: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled playsets and remove them from children. They should remove and properly dispose of the fence handles by placing them in the trash. To receive a full refund, consumers must send a photo showing the disposed fence handles to MISSJUNE at kmuyslfarmanimalrecall@gmail.com.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: The hazard involves detachable fence handles on the toy playset that can come off and pose a serious choking risk to young children, especially those under three years old. Small parts like these can block a child's airway, which can lead to serious injury or death. This risk is why the product violates federal small parts regulations designed to protect young children.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: This recall affects consumers who purchased the KMUYSL Big Red Barn Farm Animal Playsets, particularly families with children under three years old. The toy is intended for young children, but the detachable fence handles make it unsafe for this age group.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: MISSJUNE email at kmuyslfarmanimalrecall@gmail.com.

faq.answer: The fence handles on the playset can detach, creating small parts that pose a choking hazard to children under three years old.

faq.question: Where and when were the recalled playsets sold?
faq.answer: They were sold online at Amazon.com from July 2023 through June 2025 for about $20.

faq.question: How can I get a refund for the recalled KMUYSL Big Red Barn Farm Animal Playset?
faq.answer: Consumers should stop using the toy, remove and dispose of the fence handles, then send a photo of the disposed parts to kmuyslfarmanimalrecall@gmail.com to receive a full refund from MISSJUNE.

headline: Cpzzkq Baby Loungers Recalled Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Suffocation Hazard

image.caption: Recalled Cpzzkq Baby Lounger - White

description: Cpzzkq baby loungers sold on Amazon are recalled for suffocation risk due to padding that can obstruct infant breathing. Consumers should stop use and seek refund.
keywords: The recalled baby loungers violate the mandatory safety standard for infant support cushions because the padding can obstruct an infant's breathing, posing a serious risk of injury or deadly suffocation hazard.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: The Cpzzkq baby loungers sold on Amazon from October to November 2025 are recalled because their foam padding can block an infant's breathing, posing a serious suffocation hazard. Consumers should stop using these loungers immediately and contact CetoPMax for a refund.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
This recall involves Cpzzkq baby loungers, which are rectangular foam pads covered with cloth, available in white, grayish blue, pink, zuma blue, and brown. The loungers have the brand printed on a tag on the side. About 2,100 units were sold online at Amazon.com between October and November 2025 for approximately $50 each.

The loungers violate the mandatory safety standard for infant support cushions because the foam padding can obstruct an infant's breathing. This creates a serious risk of injury or death from suffocation. No injuries have been reported to date.

The loungers were distributed by Guangzhou Dida Electronic Commerce Co., Ltd., doing business as CetoPMax, based in China.

hazards: The recalled baby loungers violate the mandatory safety standard for infant support cushions because the padding can obstruct an infant's breathing, posing a serious risk of injury or deadly suffocation hazard.
remedies: Consumers should stop using the baby loungers immediately and contact CetoPMax for a refund. Consumers will be asked to remove the foam and pad from the baby lounger's cover and cut the cover, foam and pad in half. Consumers will then email photos of the destroyed pieces to?CetoPMaxRecall@outlook.com?to receive a refund.
retailers: Online at Amazon.com from October 2025 through November 2025 for about $50.
distributors: Guangzhou Dida Electronic Commerce Co., Ltd. dba CetoPMax, of China

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
To identify the recalled Cpzzkq baby loungers, look for the following:

  • Rectangular baby loungers made of a foam pad with a cloth cover.
  • Colors include white, grayish blue, pink, zuma blue, and brown.
  • The brand name "Cpzzkq" printed on a tag located on the side of the lounger.

These loungers were sold exclusively online at Amazon.com between October and November 2025.

content.subtitle: What Consumers Should Do
content.text: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled baby loungers. To receive a refund, contact CetoPMax via email at CetoPMaxRecall@outlook.com. You will be instructed to remove the foam and pad from the lounger’s cover, cut the cover, foam, and pad in half, and then email photos of the destroyed pieces to the same email address. This process ensures the product is no longer used and allows you to obtain a refund.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: The recalled baby loungers pose a suffocation hazard because the foam padding inside can block an infant's airway. Infants placed on or in these loungers may not be able to breathe properly if their nose or mouth becomes pressed against the foam. This risk is serious and can lead to injury or death. The loungers do not meet mandatory safety standards designed to prevent such hazards in infant support cushions.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: This recall affects anyone who purchased or received a Cpzzkq baby lounger sold on Amazon.com from October through November 2025. These loungers were sold for about $50 and come in multiple colors including white, grayish blue, pink, zuma blue, and brown. If you have one of these loungers, you should stop using it immediately.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: CetoPMax via email at CetoPMaxRecall@outlook.com.

headline: Recall Alert: Sweetie Baby and Style Life Eleven Baby Loungers Pose Serious Injury and Death Risks

image.caption: Recalled Style Life Eleven Baby Lounger - unzipped

description: HappyGira recalls Sweetie Baby and Style Life Eleven baby loungers due to suffocation and fall hazards violating infant sleep product standards.
keywords: Sweetie Baby and Style Life Eleven Baby Loungers

The baby loungers violate the mandatory standard for infant sleep products because the sides are shorter than the minimum side height limit to secure the infant and they do not have a stand, posing a fall hazard. Additionally, an infant can fall out of the enclosed opening at the foot of the lounger or become entrapped. Furthermore, the sleeping pad's thickness exceeds the maximum limit, posing a suffocation hazard. headline: Magnetic Stick Figure Sets Sold by BlissfulDestiny on Walmart Recalled Due to Serious Magnet Ingestion Risk

image.caption: Recalled BlissfulDestiny Magnet Men Stick Figure Set

description: BlissfulDestiny recalls magnetic stick figure sets sold on Walmart due to ingestion hazard from loose magnets posing serious injury or death risk to children.
keywords: The magnetic stick figures violate the mandatory standard for toys because they contain loose magnets posing an ingestion hazard to children. When high-powered magnets are swallowed, the ingested magnets can attract each other, or other metal objects and become lodged in the digestive system. This can result in perforations, twisting, and/or blockage of the intestines, blood poisoning and death.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: The BlissfulDestiny Magnetic Stick Figure Sets sold online at Walmart from May to October 2025 are recalled because they contain loose magnets that can be swallowed by children, causing serious internal injuries. Consumers should stop use immediately and contact BlissfulDestiny for a full refund.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
This recall involves BlissfulDestiny Magnetic Stick Figure Sets consisting of 10 flexible stick figures in various colors including purple, black, red, orange, pink, yellow, blue, white, dark green, and light green. Each figure contains four small magnets, one in each hand and foot, packaged in clear plastic.

About 140 units were sold online at Walmart.com from May 2025 through October 2025 for approximately $13. The product violates the mandatory toy safety standard due to the presence of loose magnets that pose a serious ingestion hazard to children.

No injuries have been reported to date. The sets were imported by Yiwu Shijie E-Commerce Co., Ltd., dba BlissfulDestiny, based in China.

hazards: The magnetic stick figures violate the mandatory standard for toys because they contain loose magnets posing an ingestion hazard to children. When high-powered magnets are swallowed, the ingested magnets can attract each other, or other metal objects and become lodged in the digestive system. This can result in perforations, twisting, and/or blockage of the intestines, blood poisoning and death.
remedies: Consumers should stop using the recalled magnetic stick figures immediately and take them away from children. Contact BlissfulDestiny for information on how to receive a full refund. Consumers will be asked to throw the recalled magnetic stick figures away and email a photo of the disposed product to Blissful_Destiny@outlook.com.
retailers: Online at Walmart.com from May 2025 to October 2025 for about $13.
importers: Yiwu Shijie E-Commerce Co., Ltd., dba BlissfulDestiny, of China

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
Consumers should look for sets containing 10 flexible stick figures in the following colors: purple, black, red, orange, pink, yellow, blue, white, dark green, and light green. Each figure has four small magnets—one in each hand and foot. The figures come packaged in clear plastic packaging.

These sets were sold under the BlissfulDestiny brand exclusively on Walmart.com.

content.subtitle: What Consumers Should Do
content.text:
Stop using the recalled magnetic stick figure sets immediately and keep them away from children. Consumers should dispose of the product by throwing it away and then email a photo of the disposed product to Blissful_Destiny@outlook.com.

For a full refund, contact BlissfulDestiny via the provided email address.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text:
The magnetic stick figures contain small, high-powered magnets that can detach and be swallowed by children. When more than one magnet is ingested, they can attract each other through the walls of the intestines, causing serious internal injuries such as perforations, twisting, or blockages. These injuries can lead to blood poisoning and even death if not treated promptly.

This hazard is particularly dangerous because children may not show immediate symptoms, and the magnets can cause severe damage before detection.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: This recall affects consumers who purchased the BlissfulDestiny Magnetic Stick Figure Sets online at Walmart.com between May and October 2025. The product is intended for children but poses a risk to any child who might swallow the magnets.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Email at Blissful_Destiny@outlook.com.

faq.question: What is the hazard associated with the BlissfulDestiny Magnetic Stick Figure Sets?
faq.answer: The hazard is that the small magnets in the stick figures can come loose and be swallowed by children. Swallowed magnets can attract each other inside the digestive system, causing serious injuries such as intestinal perforations, twisting, blockages, blood poisoning, or death.

faq.question: Where and when were the recalled magnetic stick figure sets sold?
faq.answer: They were sold online at Walmart.com from May 2025 through October 2025 for about $13.

faq.question: What should I do if I have the recalled magnetic stick figure set?
faq.answer: Stop using the product immediately and keep it away from children. Dispose of the set by throwing it away and email a photo of the disposed product to Blissful_Destiny@outlook.com. Contact BlissfulDestiny via the same email to receive a full refund.

headline: 32 Degrees Recalls Heated Socks Sold at Costco Because of Burn Risk

image.caption: Recalled 32 Degrees Heated Socks with rechargeable battery pack, user manual and packaging

description: 32 Degrees recalls heated socks sold at Costco due to burn hazard during high-intensity activities. Consumers should stop use and return for refund.
keywords: When worn during high-intensity activities that generate a combination of heat, excessive friction, moisture and pressure, the socks can pose an injury hazard including burns.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: 32 Degrees has recalled about 207,806 heated socks sold at Costco from August 2025 through March 2026 because they can cause burns when worn during high-intensity activities. Consumers should stop using the socks immediately and return them to Costco for a full refund.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
This recall involves 32 Degrees Heated Socks available in three sizes: medium, large, and extra large, all in black. The socks include a rechargeable battery pack labeled "32° HEAT" on the casing, packaging, user manual, and retail box. Approximately 207,806 units were sold at Costco stores and online at Costco.com between August 2025 and March 2026, priced between $30 and $46.

The socks pose a burn hazard when worn during high-intensity activities that generate heat, friction, moisture, and pressure. There have been 14 reported heat-related incidents, including 13 cases of first- or second-degree burns. Consumers are urged to stop using the socks immediately and return them to Costco for a full refund.

hazards: When worn during high-intensity activities that generate a combination of heat, excessive friction, moisture and pressure, the socks can pose an injury hazard including burns.
remedies: Consumers should immediately stop using the heated socks and return them to Costco for a full refund.
injuries: 14 heat-related incidents have been reported, with 13 involving first- or second-degree burns.
retailers: Costco stores and online at Costco.com from August 2025 through March 2026 for between $30 and $46.
importers: David Peyser Sportswear d/b/a 32 Degrees, of New York, New York

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text: To identify the recalled socks, look for black heated socks in medium, large, or extra large sizes. The battery pack casing and packaging will have the "32° HEAT" label. The user manual and exterior retail packaging also display this label. These socks were sold exclusively at Costco stores and online at Costco.com.

content.subtitle: What Consumers Should Do
content.text: Consumers should immediately stop using the heated socks to avoid burn injuries. Return the socks to any Costco store for a full refund. For more information or assistance, contact 32 Degrees toll-free at 833-997-2452 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, email recall@32degrees.com, or visit 32degrees.com/recall and click "Sock Recall" under "Support".

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: The recalled heated socks can cause burns because the combination of heat from the battery pack, friction from movement, moisture, and pressure during intense activities can lead to excessive heat buildup against the skin. This creates a risk of first- or second-degree burns, especially when worn for extended periods or during vigorous exercise.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: Consumers who purchased 32 Degrees Heated Socks in medium, large, or extra large black sizes from Costco stores or Costco.com between August 2025 and March 2026 are affected by this recall. The socks are identified by the "32° HEAT" label on the battery pack casing, packaging, user manual, and retail box.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: 32 Degrees toll-free at 833-997-2452 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, email at recall@32degrees.com, or online at https://www.32degrees.com/recall or https://www.32degrees.com and click "Sock Recall" at the bottom of the page under "Support" for more information.

faq.question: What sizes and colors of 32 Degrees Heated Socks are included in the recall?

title: 32 Degrees Recalls Heated Socks Due to Burn Hazard

editorialBrief.title: 32 Degrees Recalls Heated Socks Due to Burn Hazard
editorialBrief.headline: 32 Degrees Recalls Heated Socks Sold at Costco Because of Burn Risk
editorialBrief.metaDescription: 32 Degrees recalls heated socks sold at Costco due to burn hazard during high-intensity activities. Consumers should stop use and return for refund.
editorialBrief.quickAnswerHtml: 32 Degrees has recalled about 207,806 heated socks sold at Costco from August 2025 through March 2026 because they can cause burns when worn during high-intensity activities. Consumers should stop using the socks immediately and return them to Costco for a full refund.
editorialBrief.recallSummaryHtml:
This recall involves 32 Degrees Heated Socks available in three sizes: medium, large, and extra large, all in black. The socks include a rechargeable battery pack labeled "32° HEAT" on the casing, packaging, user manual, and retail box. Approximately 207,806 units were sold at Costco stores and online at Costco.com between August 2025 and March 2026, priced between $30 and $46.

The socks pose a burn hazard when worn during high-intensity activities that generate heat, friction, moisture, and pressure. There have been 14 reported heat-related incidents, including 13 cases of first- or second-degree burns. Consumers are urged to stop using the socks immediately and return them to Costco for a full refund.
editorialBrief.riskOverviewHtml: The recalled heated socks can cause burns because the combination of heat from the battery pack, friction from movement, moisture, and pressure during intense activities can lead to excessive heat buildup against the skin. This creates a risk of first- or second-degree burns, especially when worn for extended periods or during vigorous exercise.
editorialBrief.whoIsAffectedHtml: Consumers who purchased 32 Degrees Heated Socks in medium, large, or extra large black sizes from Costco stores or Costco.com between August 2025 and March 2026 are affected by this recall. The socks are identified by the "32° HEAT" label on the battery pack casing, packaging, user manual, and retail box.
editorialBrief.howToIdentifyProductHtml: To identify the recalled socks, look for black heated socks in medium, large, or extra large sizes. The battery pack casing and packaging will have the "32° HEAT" label. The user manual and exterior retail packaging also display this label. These socks were sold exclusively at Costco stores and online at Costco.com.
editorialBrief.whatToDoHtml: Consumers should immediately stop using the heated socks to avoid burn injuries. Return the socks to any Costco store for a full refund. For more information or assistance, contact 32 Degrees toll-free at 833-997-2452 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, email recall@32degrees.com, or visit 32degrees.com/recall and click "Sock Recall" under "Support".
